questions.question: In what year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: WWII ended with Japan's surrender on September 2, 1945.

questions.question: Who was the first President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Washington served as president from 1789 to 1797.

questions.question: What ancient civilization built the pyramids of Giza?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid was built around 2560 BCE for Pharaoh Khufu.

questions.question: What year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.

questions.question: Who was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic?
questions.explanation: Earhart made the solo flight in 1932.

questions.question: The Magna Carta was signed in which year?
questions.explanation: King John sealed the Magna Carta at Runnymede on June 15, 1215.

questions.question: What was the Treaty of Westphalia (1648) primarily about?
questions.explanation: The treaty established the concept of state sovereignty.

questions.question: Who was the last pharaoh of ancient Egypt?
questions.explanation: Cleopatra VII ruled until her death in 30 BCE.

questions.question: What empire was ruled by Suleiman the Magnificent?
questions.explanation: Suleiman ruled the Ottoman Empire from 1520 to 1566.

questions.question: What ship sank on its maiden voyage in 1912?
questions.explanation: The Titanic sank after hitting an iceberg on April 15, 1912.

questions.question: What was the name of the first man to walk on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Armstrong stepped onto the Moon on July 20, 1969.

questions.question: Which country gifted the Statue of Liberty to the USA?
questions.explanation: France gifted the statue in 1886 to celebrate American independence.

questions.question: What year did the American Civil War begin?
questions.explanation: The Civil War began with the attack on Fort Sumter on April 12, 1861.

questions.question: Who discovered America in 1492?
questions.explanation: Columbus reached the Americas on October 12, 1492.

questions.question: What ancient wonder was located in Alexandria, Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Lighthouse of Alexandria was one of the tallest structures of the ancient world.

questions.question: Who painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel?
questions.explanation: Michelangelo painted the ceiling between 1508 and 1512.

questions.question: What event triggered World War I?
questions.explanation: The assassination in Sarajevo on June 28, 1914 sparked the war.

questions.question: Who led India's independence movement through nonviolence?
questions.explanation: Gandhi's philosophy of nonviolent resistance helped achieve Indian independence in 1947.

questions.question: What was the Manhattan Project?
questions.explanation: The secret project produced the first nuclear weapons during WWII.

questions.question: Which dynasty built the Great Wall of China?
questions.explanation: Emperor Qin Shi Huang began the wall around 221 BCE, though later dynasties extended it.

questions.question: What year did the French Revolution begin?
questions.explanation: The storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789 marked the revolution's start.

questions.question: Who was the first Emperor of Rome?
questions.explanation: Augustus (Octavian) became the first emperor in 27 BCE.

questions.question: What was the Cold War?
questions.correctAnswer: Geopolitical tension between USA and USSR
incorrectAnswers: A war fought in the Arctic
A trade dispute between Europe and Asia
questions.explanation: The Cold War lasted from roughly 1947 to 1991.

questions.question: Who wrote the Declaration of Independence?
questions.explanation: Jefferson was the primary author in 1776.

questions.question: What ancient city was destroyed by Mount Vesuvius?
questions.explanation: Vesuvius erupted in 79 CE, burying Pompeii under volcanic ash.

questions.question: What was the Silk Road?
questions.correctAnswer: Ancient trade route connecting East and West
questions.explanation: The Silk Road facilitated trade and cultural exchange for centuries.

questions.question: Who founded the Mongol Empire?
questions.explanation: Genghis Khan united the Mongol tribes and created the largest contiguous empire.

questions.question: What was the Reconquista?
questions.correctAnswer: Christian reconquest of the Iberian Peninsula
questions.explanation: The Reconquista ended in 1492 with the fall of Granada.

questions.question: What year did Constantinople fall to the Ottomans?
questions.explanation: The fall of Constantinople marked the end of the Byzantine Empire.

questions.question: Who was Hannibal Barca?
questions.correctAnswer: Carthaginian general who crossed the Alps
questions.explanation: Hannibal famously crossed the Alps with elephants to attack Rome.

questions.question: What year did humans first land on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969.

questions.question: Who was known as the Iron Lady?
questions.explanation: Thatcher was the UK Prime Minister from 1979 to 1990.

questions.question: What civilization invented democracy?
questions.explanation: Athens developed the first known democracy around 508 BCE.

questions.question: What was the Renaissance?
questions.correctAnswer: A cultural rebirth in Europe from the 14th-17th century
questions.explanation: The Renaissance began in Italy and spread across Europe.

questions.question: Who was the first female Prime Minister of the UK?
questions.explanation: Thatcher took office in 1979.

questions.question: What was apartheid?
questions.correctAnswer: A system of racial segregation in South Africa
incorrectAnswers: A form of government in India
questions.explanation: Apartheid lasted from 1948 to 1991 in South Africa.

questions.question: Who led the Soviet Union during most of World War II?
questions.explanation: Stalin led the USSR from 1924 until his death in 1953.

questions.question: What was the Meiji Restoration?
questions.explanation: The Meiji Restoration ended feudalism and rapidly industrialized Japan.

questions.question: Who was Spartacus?
questions.correctAnswer: A gladiator who led a slave revolt against Rome
questions.explanation: Spartacus led the Third Servile War from 73-71 BCE.

questions.question: What was the Scramble for Africa?
questions.correctAnswer: European colonization of Africa in the late 1800s
incorrectAnswers: A gold rush in South Africa
questions.explanation: European powers divided Africa at the Berlin Conference of 1884-85.

questions.question: What was the Great Fire of London?
questions.explanation: The fire destroyed much of the medieval City of London.

questions.question: Who was Alexander the Great?
questions.correctAnswer: A Macedonian king who built a vast empire
questions.explanation: Alexander conquered much of the known world by age 30.

questions.question: What was the Black Death?
questions.correctAnswer: A plague pandemic in the 14th century
questions.explanation: The Black Death killed an estimated 25-50 million Europeans.

questions.question: Who invented the printing press?
questions.explanation: Gutenberg developed movable type printing around 1440.

questions.question: What was the Rosetta Stone's significance?
questions.explanation: The stone had the same text in hieroglyphics, Demotic, and Greek.

questions.question: What was the Enlightenment?
questions.explanation: The Enlightenment influenced modern democracy, science, and philosophy.

questions.question: What country was Adolf Hitler the leader of?
questions.explanation: Hitler led Nazi Germany from 1933 to 1945.

questions.question: Who was the first woman in space?
questions.explanation: Tereshkova orbited Earth on June 16, 1963.

questions.question: What was the Balfour Declaration?
questions.correctAnswer: British support for a Jewish homeland in Palestine
questions.explanation: Issued in 1917, it played a key role in the creation of Israel.

questions.question: What ancient writing system used wedge-shaped marks?
questions.explanation: Cuneiform was developed by the Sumerians around 3400 BCE.
